title: IP Filing Assistant — Patents, Trademarks & Copyright
tagline: Protect your innovation, brand, and creative work with India's intellectual property system.

patent.title: Patent Filing in India
patent.description: A patent gives you exclusive right to make, use, and sell your invention for 20 years from filing date. Blocks competitors from copying your core technology.

types.name: Provisional Patent Application
types.purpose: Secures your 'priority date' cheaply while you refine the invention. Gives 12 months to file complete specification.
types.timeline: Instant filing — date secured same day
types.validity: Not a grant; establishes priority date only
types.when_to_use: As soon as you have a novel idea/prototype, before any public disclosure

types.name: Complete Patent Application
types.purpose: Full patent application claiming all claims. Can be converted from provisional within 12 months.
types.timeline: 18 months publication + examination time = 4-6 years to grant (without expedited)
types.validity: 20 years from filing date if granted
types.when_to_use: After refining full claims; convert from provisional within 12 months

types.name: Expedited (Request for Examination - Form 18A)
types.purpose: Fast-track examination of your patent application. Used by DPIIT-recognised startups.
types.timeline: Examination typically within 6-12 months of request
types.when_to_use: DPIIT startups should file RFE (Form 18) within 48 months of filing to trigger examination

process: Prior art search: Check if similar patents exist on Google Patents, Indian Patent Office (ipindia.gov.in), USPTO
Draft patent specification: Abstract, description, drawings, claims (hire a patent agent for complex inventions)
File on ipindia.gov.in: Create account → Select 'Application for Patent' → Upload PDF specification
Pay government filing fee online (₹1,750 for startup provisional)
Get Patent Application Number immediately — use this as 'Patent Pending' in marketing
Publication: Patent published in Patent Journal 18 months after priority date (can request early publication at ₹2,500)
Request for Examination (Form 18/18A): File within 48 months of priority date to start examination
Examination: First Examination Report (FER) issued — respond to objections within 6-12 months
Grant: If all objections cleared, patent granted and published in Patent Journal
dpiit_benefits: 80% rebate on all patent fees (file fee, examination fee, renewal fee)
IP facilitation centres at several incubators (IIMB, IIT Delhi, etc.) for free guidance

costs_summary.filing_self: ₹1,750 – ₹10,000 (government fee only — not recommended without IP expertise)
costs_summary.renewal_per_year: ₹800 – ₹1,600 after grant (years 1-5); higher for later years

what_is_patentable: New use of an existing compound
Software if it solves a technical problem (not pure algorithm or business method)
not_patentable: Computer program per se (but embedded in hardware — possibly)

attorney_finder.description: Find registered Indian patent agents (advocates) on the official list
attorney_finder.avg_cost: ₹20,000 – ₹50,000 for drafting + filing a technical invention

trademark.title: Trademark Registration in India
trademark.description: A trademark protects your brand name, logo, tagline, or distinctive mark. Prevents competitors from using confusingly similar marks in your class of goods/services.

types.name: Word Mark
types.description: Protects the text/words of your brand name alone (e.g., 'Razorpay', 'Mamaearth')
types.recommendation: File first — protects the name regardless of font/design

types.name: Device/Logo Mark
types.description: Protects a specific logo or design. Can be combined with words.

types.name: Sound/Shape/Color Mark
types.description: Unconventional marks. Harder to register but possible (Intel jingle, Louboutin red sole).

nice_classes.description: Software, apps, scientific apparatus, electronic devices
Business management, advertising, retail services, B2B services
IT services, SaaS, technology design, cloud computing
Legal services, personal security, social services, LegalTech
Clothing, footwear, headgear — for D2C fashion brands
Cosmetics, cleaning products, personal care — for D2C beauty brands
Pharmaceutical, dietary supplements, baby food, medical preparations
Food (tea, coffee, spices, packaged food) — for D2C food brands
Processed food, dairy, meat, fish — for food startups

process: Trademark search: Search ipindiaservices.gov.in for identical/similar marks in your class(es)
Assess similarity: Check for phonetic, visual, and conceptual similarity with existing marks
Prepare application: Drafting on ipindiaservices.gov.in — name, class, proprietor details, user evidence
File application: Upload on ipindiaservices.gov.in; pay government fee
TM number issued immediately: Your mark is now 'TM' pending (use ™ symbol)
Examination: Trademark Registry examines within 3-6 months; issues Examination Report
Response: Respond to objections within 30 days
Publication in Trademark Journal: Third parties can oppose within 4 months
Registration: If no opposition (or opposition resolved in your favor), certificate issued in 18-24 months
Use ® symbol only after registration certificate
trademark.timeline: 18-24 months to full registration. ™ usage allowed immediately on filing.
trademark.dpiit_benefit: DPIIT startups get 50% rebate on government trademark filing fees
trademark.renewal: Valid for 10 years from application date; renewable for ₹9,000 per class (company)
tips: File in multiple classes if your brand extends across product types (e.g., Mamaearth files in Classes 3, 5, 25, 35, 44)
File as a company, not in personal name — ownership stays with business
DPIIT recognised startups get 50% fee rebate — register DPIIT first
Conduct trademark search before finalizing your brand name — saves expensive rebranding later

copyright.title: Copyright Registration in India
copyright.description: Copyright protects original creative works — software code, written content, music, art, films. In India, copyright is automatic on creation, but registration provides legal proof and simplified enforcement.
what_is_protected: Computer source code and object code
Original literary works (blog posts, whitepapers, reports)
Artistic works (logo art, UI designs, illustrations)
what_is_not_protected: Ideas (only expression is protected, not ideas)
Titles and short phrases (use trademark for these)
process: File at copyright.gov.in (Copyright Office, India)
Create account on official portal
Select work type: Literary / Computer Program / Artistic / Musical / Film / Sound Recording
Fill Form XIV (computer programs) or Form XIII (literary works) online
Upload the work in PDF/digital format
Pay government fee (₹500 for literary/software; ₹5,000 for cinematograph film)
A waiting period of 30 days: any third-party objections are invited
If no objection: Copyright Diary Number issued; then Copyright Registration Certificate in 3-4 months
copyright.timeline: 3-4 months for registration. Copyright exists from creation date, not registration date.
copyright.validity: Life of creator + 60 years for literary/artistic works. 60 years for films/sound recordings from publication.
tips: Always add copyright notice © [Year] [Company Name] to your code, content, and designs
Register your software's source code — provides court evidence in infringement disputes
Register your website UI/UX designs as 'Artistic Work' to protect visual design from copying
Copyright registration is very cheap (₹500) — register every major product version
